title: High temperature radio frequency identification (rfid) tag
abstract: High temperature radio frequency identification (RFID) tags are formed from nesting insulative ceramic structures with a woven cladding provided around an RFID tag. Additional interstitial woven cladding may be positioned between the ceramic structures. The layered approach provides sufficient insulation that allows sustained operation at temperatures above 500-600 degrees centigrade (500-600Â°C) while being sufficiently transparent to radio frequency (RF) signals to allow interrogation and response for track and trace purposes even at such elevated temperatures.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1 A method of using a radio frequency identification (RFID) tag, comprising; conducting sustained operations with the RFID tag while the RFID tag is exposed to temperatures above 450 degrees centigrade (450Â°C) 2 The method of claim 1, wherein the temperature is above 500Â°C. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the temperature is above 550Â°C. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the temperature is above 600Â°C. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein conducting the sustained operations comprises interrogating the RFID tag and receiving coherent responses therefrom for more than ten minutes at the temperatures above 450Â°C. 6 The method of claim 1 , wherein conducting the sustained operations comprises interrogating the RFID tag and receiving coherent responses therefrom for more than thirty minutes at the temperatures above 450Â°C. 7 The method of claim 1, wherein conducting the sustained operations comprises interrogating the RFID tag and receiving coherent responses therefrom for more than fifty minutes at the temperatures above 450Â°C. 8. An apparatus comprising: a radio frequency identification (RFID) tag, a first woven fabric cladding surrounding the RFID tag a first ceramic housing encapsulating the first woven fabric cladding, a second woven fabric cladding surrounding the first ceramic housing; and a second ceramic housing encapsulating the second woven fabric cladding. 9. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the first woven fabric cladding comprises a woven silica cloth. 10. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the first woven fabric cladding surrounding the RFID tag comprises an air barrier for the RFID tag. 1 1. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the first woven fabric cladding surrounding the RFID tag comprises a generally cylindrical shape. 12. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the first ceramic housing comprises a Cotronics ceramic resin.
